cases.query: How has the transition to the Unity Catalog model registry affected your workflow?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a reliable migration tool that preserves line
As a Data Analyst, I want to search for tables by column names and descriptions

pain_points.description: The transition to the Unity Catalog model registry disrupted CI/CD tooling due to changes in model URI format, leading to broken references across notebooks and pipelines.
Loss of lineage history during migration from the old metastore to Unity Catalog, requiring manual reconstruction for compliance documentation.
Weak search capabilities in Unity Catalog explorer, limiting analysts' ability to find tables by column names or descriptions.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have a reliable migration tool that preserves lineage information during the transition to Unity Catalog, so that I can maintain compliance documentation without manual reconstruction.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of losing important lineage data during migration, which is critical for compliance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to search for tables by column names and descriptions in Unity Catalog, so that I can quickly find relevant data without relying on others.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of inefficient data discovery due to weak search capabilities.

cases.summary: The transition to the Unity Catalog model registry has caused significant disruptions in workflows, particularly due to broken CI/CD tooling and loss of lineage history during migration. Additionally, the search functionality in Unity Catalog is inadequate, hindering data discovery for analysts.

cases.query: Can you describe any performance issues you're facing when using Databricks SQL?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex mo
As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or budget alerts on serverless wareh
As a Data Analyst, I want reliable dashboard refresh notifications, so that I ca
As a Data Analyst, I want collaborative editing features in the SQL editor, so t

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models can take 20-30 seconds before any data is processed.
Cost unpredictability with serverless warehouses can lead to unexpectedly high expenses.
JDBC connectivity issues cause random drops in Tableau connections to Databricks SQL.
Dashboard refresh jobs can fail silently, leading to data discrepancies.
Lack of collaboration features in the SQL editor can lead to data loss.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to reduce query compilation time for complex models, so that I can iterate on my development faster without long wait times.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the significant overhead in query compilation time that slows down development iterations.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to set cost caps or budget alerts on serverless warehouses, so that I can manage and predict expenses more effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of cost unpredictability with serverless warehouses.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want reliable dashboard refresh notifications, so that I can ensure data accuracy for executive reports.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of silent failures in dashboard refresh jobs that lead to discrepancies.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want collaborative editing features in the SQL editor, so that I can work simultaneously with my team without losing changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the risk of data loss due to lack of collaboration features in the SQL editor.

cases.summary: Users are experiencing significant performance issues with query compilation times and unpredictable costs with serverless warehouses. Additionally, there are concerns about dashboard reliability and collaboration in the SQL editor, which can lead to data discrepancies and loss.

cases.query: How do analysts feel about the performance of their queries?

per_story.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language summary of why my query was slow, so

pain_points.description: Query performance variance leads to unpredictable execution times, causing frustration among analysts.
Lack of visibility into query performance factors prevents analysts from diagnosing and improving their queries.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want a plain-language summary of why my query was slow, so that I can understand and improve my query performance without needing to read complex Spark plans.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for analysts to have clear insights into query performance issues, which is currently a significant pain point.

cases.summary: Analysts are experiencing significant frustration due to unpredictable query performance, with execution times varying drastically for the same queries. Additionally, the lack of clear explanations for slow queries hinders their ability to optimize performance effectively.

cases.query: What are the key pain points in cost management for MLflow artifacts?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define artifact retention policies for MLflow exp
As an ML Engineer, I want a bulk management UI for experiments in MLflow, so tha
As a Platform Admin, I want an artifact storage dashboard in MLflow, so that I c

pain_points.description: Artifacts from abandoned experiments accumulate indefinitely, leading to high storage costs.
MLflow lacks native retention policies, requiring manual deletion of artifacts and experiments.
There is no UI for bulk deletion of experiments, making cleanup cumbersome.
Storage costs for MLflow artifacts are not clearly attributed to experiments in cost reporting.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define artifact retention policies for MLflow experiments, so that I can automatically clean up stale artifacts and manage storage costs effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for automated management of artifact storage to prevent unnecessary costs.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a bulk management UI for experiments in MLflow, so that I can easily delete or archive multiple experiments without using the API.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual cleanup processes that are currently inefficient and time-consuming.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want an artifact storage dashboard in MLflow, so that I can see storage consumption and costs attributed to each experiment.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the lack of visibility into storage costs, enabling better cost management.

cases.summary: The key pain points in cost management for MLflow artifacts include the accumulation of artifacts from abandoned experiments, the absence of native retention policies, and the lack of a user-friendly interface for bulk deletion. Additionally, storage costs are not clearly attributed to experiments, complicating cost visibility and management. Addressing these issues through defined retention policies and improved UI features can significantly reduce unnecessary storage expenses.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to DLT pipeline performance and monitoring?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform a selective refresh on DLT pipelines, so t
As a Data Engineer, I want to receive actionable error diagnostics in the DLT ev
As a Data Engineer, I want to have local testing capabilities for DLT transforma
As a Data Engineer, I want to receive push notifications for DLT pipeline health
As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation on watermarking and late data ha
As a Data Engineer, I want alerts for continuous max cluster size usage, so that

pain_points.description: Full refresh of DLT pipelines drops and recreates all tables, causing hours of reprocessing and slowing down iteration speed.
DLT pipeline failures often provide cryptic error messages, making it difficult to diagnose issues without opening support tickets.
Lack of local testing capabilities for DLT expectations and transformations forces engineers to spin up cloud compute for testing.
Monitoring of DLT pipeline health is manual, requiring engineers to check logs regularly without proactive alerts.
Watermarking and late data handling issues lead to silent drops of late-arriving events without warnings in the DLT event log.
Cluster costs can spike unexpectedly due to configuration changes, with no alerts for continuous max cluster size usage.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to perform a selective refresh on DLT pipelines, so that I can update specific tables without causing downtime for the entire pipeline.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of long reprocessing times and operational delays caused by full refreshes.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive actionable error diagnostics in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly identify the root cause of pipeline failures without needing to open support tickets.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of cryptic error messages that hinder timely issue resolution.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to have local testing capabilities for DLT transformations, so that I can validate changes without incurring cloud compute costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of expensive testing processes that currently require cloud resources.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to receive push notifications for DLT pipeline health, so that I can be alerted to issues without manually checking logs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the inefficiency of manual monitoring and the need for proactive alerts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation on watermarking and late data handling in DLT, so that I can avoid silent drops of late-arriving events.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the challenge of diagnosing data discrepancies caused by improper watermark settings.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want alerts for continuous max cluster size usage, so that I can manage cluster costs effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the risk of unexpected cost spikes due to configuration changes.

cases.summary: Key pain points include the lengthy reprocessing times due to full refreshes of DLT pipelines, cryptic error messages that complicate failure diagnosis, and the lack of local testing capabilities. Additionally, manual monitoring and unexpected cost spikes highlight the need for improved operational efficiency and proactive alerts.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have regarding the lineage graph in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all work
As a Platform Admin, I want to run impact analysis from any table or column node

pain_points.description: The lineage graph stops at workspace boundaries, preventing users from tracing data lineage across multiple workspaces.
Column-level lineage is not captured for all operation types, leading to incomplete lineage information.
There is no automated way to perform blast-radius analysis for upstream schema changes, making it difficult to assess the impact on downstream data.
Data stewards cannot easily answer compliance questions about data flow without manual investigation across multiple workspaces.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to trace a column's complete lineage across all workspaces in the account, so that I can assess the blast radius of upstream schema changes and answer compliance questions about data flow.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for comprehensive lineage tracking across workspaces, which is crucial for data governance and compliance.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to run impact analysis from any table or column node in the lineage graph, so that I can quickly identify all downstream tables, views, and dashboards affected by changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the lack of automated blast-radius analysis, enabling faster decision-making and risk assessment.

cases.summary: Users have identified significant pain points regarding the lineage graph in Databricks, particularly its inability to track lineage across multiple workspaces and the lack of automated impact analysis. These issues hinder data governance and compliance efforts, prompting requests for enhanced lineage capabilities.

cases.query: How does the privilege model in Unity Catalog affect your team’s workflow?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to automate the tagging of migrated tables in Unity C
As a Platform Engineer, I want stronger metadata isolation in Unity Catalog, so
As an ML Engineer, I want a built-in approval workflow for model promotions in U

pain_points.description: Migration from Hive metastore to Unity Catalog is challenging due to immature tooling and manual re-tagging of tables.
Unity Catalog does not provide sufficient metadata isolation, leading to potential data leakage between clients.
Current model promotion process lacks an approval workflow, leaving no traceable audit record for compliance.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to automate the tagging of migrated tables in Unity Catalog, so that I can reduce the manual effort required for data governance post-migration.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of having to manually re-tag tables after migration, which is time-consuming and error-prone.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Engineer, I want stronger metadata isolation in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure compliance with data isolation requirements for different clients.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the concern of potential metadata leakage that could compromise client data security.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a built-in approval workflow for model promotions in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and maintain an audit trail.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a documented approval process to satisfy compliance audits.

cases.summary: The transition to Unity Catalog presents significant challenges, particularly in migration and compliance. Key pain points include the need for improved tooling for table migration, stronger metadata isolation to prevent data leakage, and the implementation of an approval workflow for model promotions to meet regulatory standards.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to output quality evaluation in the Mosaic AI Playground?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to automate output quality evaluation with statistical
As an ML Engineer, I want to manage ground truth datasets with versioning in Uni

pain_points.description: Output quality evaluation is manual and lacks statistical tracking.
No systematic way to evaluate agent quality beyond manual spot-checking outputs.
Ground truth dataset management is ad hoc with no versioning or governance.
Evaluation results are not tracked in MLflow alongside model metrics.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to automate output quality evaluation with statistical tracking, so that I can reliably measure and improve agent performance without manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual evaluation and the need for systematic quality measurement.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want to manage ground truth datasets with versioning in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure consistent evaluation across experiments.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of ad hoc dataset management and the need for governance.

cases.summary: Key pain points in the Mosaic AI Playground include the manual nature of output quality evaluation and the lack of systematic tracking for agent quality. Additionally, ground truth dataset management is inconsistent, highlighting the need for better governance and integration with MLflow.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have for improving data governance in Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descri
As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce a tag taxonomy for feature tables in Unit
As a Data Scientist, I want duplicate detection on feature creation in Unity Cat
As a Platform Admin, I want to improve the lineage tracking in Unity Catalog to

pain_points.description: Current Unity Catalog search only matches table names, not column names, descriptions, or tags, leading to duplicate feature creation.
Feature tables lack enforced description or tag taxonomy, resulting in empty descriptions that hinder effective search.
No duplicate detection when creating new features, causing redundant feature engineering efforts.
Lineage data has gaps for certain operations, making it difficult to trace data flow for compliance purposes.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descriptions, and tags in Unity Catalog, so that I can find relevant existing features in under 2 minutes and eliminate duplicate feature engineering.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of duplicate feature creation due to inadequate search capabilities.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce a tag taxonomy for feature tables in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure all features are properly documented and searchable.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of empty descriptions that hinder effective feature discoverability.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want duplicate detection on feature creation in Unity Catalog, so that I can be warned about existing similar features and avoid redundant work.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly tackles the problem of wasted engineering time on duplicate features.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to improve the lineage tracking in Unity Catalog to capture all operations, so that I can provide complete data flow visibility for compliance audits.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the gaps in lineage data that complicate compliance reporting.

cases.summary: Users have highlighted significant pain points regarding data governance in Unity Catalog, particularly around feature discoverability and lineage tracking. Key requests include enhanced search capabilities, enforced tagging, and improved lineage tracking to support compliance needs.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding model promotion documentation?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Mode

pain_points.description: Current model promotion process lacks a formal approval workflow, leading to compliance issues and no audit trail.
Promotion events do not record reviewer identity or criteria evaluated, making it difficult to demonstrate compliance.
Any user with EXECUTE privilege can promote models without required review, posing governance risks.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Model Registry, so that I can ensure all model promotions are reviewed and documented for compliance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a formal approval process to meet regulatory requirements and provide an audit trail.

cases.summary: Users in regulated industries face significant challenges with the current model promotion process in Databricks, as it lacks necessary approval workflows and audit trails required for compliance. The absence of formal documentation and review mechanisms poses governance risks, leading to potential issues during audits.

cases.query: How can Databricks improve the alerting system for job monitoring?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to configure built-in SLA alerting for Auto Loader st

pain_points.description: Lack of built-in SLA alerting for Auto Loader streams leads to manual monitoring setups that are fragile and time-consuming.
Current monitoring jobs can fail silently, resulting in undetected stalled streams.
No event-driven alert mechanism for stalled streaming pipelines, requiring polling of the stream progress API.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to configure built-in SLA alerting for Auto Loader streams, so that I can receive timely notifications if no files are processed within a specified time window without relying on fragile custom monitoring jobs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of time-consuming and unreliable monitoring setups, allowing for more efficient management of ingestion streams.

cases.summary: The current alerting system for job monitoring in Databricks lacks built-in SLA configurations for Auto Loader streams, leading to inefficient and fragile manual setups. Data Engineers face significant challenges with undetected stalled streams and the need for a more reliable, event-driven alert mechanism.

cases.query: What feature requests do Data Engineers have for improving error diagnostics in DLT?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and detailed error messages in
As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after p
As a Data Engineer, I want the DLT event log to include the original exception c

pain_points.description: DLT pipelines fail with cryptic runtime errors, making diagnosis difficult without support tickets.
DLT executor logs are not persisted after cluster termination, leading to loss of critical diagnostic information.
Error messages for common failure modes are generic and do not provide actionable insights.
The DLT event log lacks detailed information such as original exception class and stack trace.
Documentation on watermarking and late data handling is insufficient, leading to undetected data discrepancies.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and detailed error messages in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly diagnose and resolve pipeline failures without needing to open a support ticket.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for actionable insights to reduce downtime and improve trust in pipeline outputs.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want DLT executor logs to be persisted for 30 days after pipeline termination, so that I can investigate failures without losing critical log information.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of lost logs that hinder troubleshooting efforts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want the DLT event log to include the original exception class and stack trace, so that I can understand the root cause of failures more effectively.
user_stories.rationale: This story aims to provide deeper insights into errors, facilitating faster diagnosis.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with error diagnostics in DLT due to cryptic error messages, lack of persisted logs, and insufficient detail in event logs. Structured error codes and improved logging features are critical for enabling self-diagnosis and reducing reliance on support tickets.

cases.query: What issues did the attendees highlight about the SCIM synchronization process?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want the SCIM synchronization process to reliably deactiv

pain_points.description: The SCIM sync process fails intermittently, causing deprovisioned users to retain access for 4-6 hours due to internal server errors.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for timely user deactivation to prevent security incidents and meet compliance standards.

cases.summary: Attendees highlighted significant issues with the SCIM synchronization process, particularly its failure to promptly deactivate users, which poses security risks and compliance challenges. The need for reliable user deactivation mechanisms is critical for maintaining security and meeting audit requirements.

cases.query: What feature requests do users have to improve their experience with notebook environment management?

As a Data Scientist, I want to resume my notebook session with all packages and

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want native real-time collaboration in notebooks, so that my team can work together without the risk of overwriting each other's changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for effective teamwork and reduces the risk of data loss during collaborative efforts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to resume my notebook session with all packages and state restored, so that I can start working immediately without manual setup.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of losing in-memory state due to cluster termination, significantly improving productivity.

cases.summary: Users express a strong need for improved notebook environment management, particularly in real-time collaboration and state persistence. pain_points.description: Non-additive schema changes cause stream failures with minimal diagnostic information.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better handling of schema changes to prevent unplanned stream failures.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant challenges with Auto Loader due to unhandled non-additive schema changes that lead to stream failures. The lack of proactive alerts and clear diagnostics exacerbates the issue, highlighting the need for improved schema change management features.

cases.query: What user needs are unmet regarding production LLM application quality?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an evaluation harness for LLM agents that integrates w

cases.query: What challenges did Amara face when updating cluster policies across multiple workspaces?

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of having to make multiple individual updates for each workspace, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

cases.summary: Amara faced significant challenges in managing cluster policies across multiple workspaces, as updates had to be made individually for each workspace. This inefficiency highlights the need for centralized management capabilities to streamline administrative tasks and reduce operational overhead.

cases.query: What challenges do data professionals encounter with automated classification of PII/PHI?

As a Platform Admin, I want to define policy templates tied to classification ta

Initial classification of tables and columns is manual, with no automated scanning for PII candidates.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the challenge of manual policy enforcement and enhances compliance management.

cases.summary: Data professionals face significant challenges with the manual processes involved in classifying and enforcing policies for PII/PHI in Databricks. The lack of automation in both initial classification and policy enforcement leads to increased compliance risks and operational inefficiencies. Implementing automated classification and policy templates could greatly alleviate these pain points.

cases.summary: Data scientists face significant frustrations with the Feature Store, particularly around feature discoverability and the risk of duplicating existing features. The lack of a consistent tagging system and built-in validation for data leakage further complicates their workflows. Addressing these pain points through enhanced search capabilities and validation mechanisms is crucial for improving the overall utility of the Feature Store.

cases.summary: ML engineers face significant challenges in evaluating LLM agents due to the lack of systematic evaluation tools and integration with MLflow. user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a more robust and maintainable way to handle task dependencies and execution logic.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to manage cross-workspace task dependencies natively in Databricks Workflows, so that I can eliminate the latency and fragility of polling workarounds.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the need for improved orchestration capabilities across multiple workspaces, enhancing pipeline efficiency.

cases.summary: Key unmet needs in workflow orchestration for Databricks include the lack of support for cross-workspace task dependencies and the reliance on fragile workarounds for conditional task execution. Addressing these pain points would significantly enhance the reliability and efficiency of data pipelines.

cases.query: What unmet needs do Data Engineers have when dealing with Delta Live Tables errors?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and actionable diagnostics in
As a Data Engineer, I want to access persisted executor logs for 30 days after p
As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation on watermarking and late data ha

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding temporal validation in feature lookups?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an optional temporal validation parameter in FeatureLo
As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to surface in training set statistic

pain_points.description: The Feature Store does not validate whether joined feature values predate the event timestamp, leading to silent data leakage issues.
Data leakage from look-ahead bias is a common failure pattern in supervised learning, yet the Feature Store lacks a detection layer.
Post-hoc leakage detection requires custom code, which most teams do not implement until after a production incident.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want an optional temporal validation parameter in FeatureLookup that warns when feature values consistently postdate the lookup timestamp, so that I can prevent data leakage before deploying models.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for proactive validation to avoid silent data leakage issues that degrade model performance.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to surface in training set statistics whether any feature columns contain future values relative to the label timestamp, so that I can ensure the integrity of my training data.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly addresses the pain point of undetected future-state features that can lead to model quality failures.

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges with temporal validation in feature lookups, leading to data leakage and degraded model performance. The lack of proactive validation mechanisms in the Feature Store results in silent failures that are only discovered post-deployment. Implementing temporal validation features could greatly enhance the reliability of training data and prevent future issues.

cases.query: How do Data Engineers feel about the current error messages in DLT pipelines?

DLT executor logs are not persisted after cluster termination, preventing engineers from accessing crucial diagnostic information.
Common failure modes are surfaced as the same generic runtime error string, leading to confusion and prolonged downtime.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want structured error codes and human-readable summaries in the DLT event log, so that I can quickly identify the root cause of pipeline failures without opening a support ticket.

cases.summary: Data Engineers express significant frustration with the current error handling in DLT pipelines, citing cryptic error messages and the lack of persisted logs as major pain points. These issues lead to prolonged downtime and hinder the ability to self-diagnose failures, emphasizing the need for structured error diagnostics and improved log retention.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations experienced by ML Engineers using the Databricks Feature Store?

pain_points.description: Point-in-time lookups in the Feature Store can lead to data leakage issues without proper validation.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of poor discoverability, which leads to inefficiencies in feature engineering.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want the Feature Store to provide native monitoring and alerting for feature distribution shifts, so that I can proactively manage model performance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the lack of monitoring capabilities that could help in maintaining model accuracy over time.

cases.summary: ML Engineers face significant frustrations with the Databricks Feature Store, particularly around data leakage due to inadequate validation for point-in-time joins and poor feature discoverability. Additionally, the absence of built-in monitoring for feature distributions hampers proactive model management. Addressing these issues could greatly enhance the usability and reliability of the Feature Store.

cases.query: What pain points do users experience with the current SQL editor in Databricks?

Unpredictable costs associated with serverless SQL warehouses create stress for users managing budgets.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want conflict detection in the SQL editor, so that I can collaborate on queries without losing my work due to silent overwrites.

cases.summary: Users experience significant pain points with the SQL editor in Databricks, particularly around collaboration and version control, leading to lost work and inefficiencies. Additionally, performance issues and cost unpredictability with SQL warehouses further complicate the user experience. Enhancements in conflict detection and version history are critical for improving collaboration and productivity.

cases.summary: The key pain points around conditional branching implementation in Databricks Workflows include reliance on fragile hacks for task execution and lack of visibility into task execution paths. cases.query: What challenges do ML engineers face when evaluating their LLM-powered agents?

cases.summary: ML engineers face significant challenges in evaluating LLM-powered agents due to a lack of systematic evaluation frameworks and manual processes for tracking quality. cases.query: What frustrations do Data Engineers have when handling schema changes in Auto Loader?

As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to provide detailed error messages that s

user_stories.rationale: This story targets the need for better handling of schema changes to avoid stream failures.

cases.summary: Data Engineers face significant frustrations with Auto Loader when handling schema changes, particularly due to stream failures caused by non-additive changes and a lack of proactive alerts. pain_points.description: Unity Catalog's search capabilities are limited to table names, preventing users from searching by column names, descriptions, or tags.
Many feature tables lack enforced descriptions or tag taxonomies, resulting in empty descriptions that render text search ineffective.
Data scientists frequently recreate existing features due to the inability to discover them, leading to wasted engineering time.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descriptions, and tags in Unity Catalog, so that I can quickly find relevant existing features and avoid duplicating work.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for efficient feature discovery, which is currently hindered by the limitations of the search functionality.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want a 'similar features' suggestion when creating a new feature definition, so that I can be alerted to existing features and prevent redundancy.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly tackles the issue of duplicate feature creation, which is a significant pain point for users.

cases.summary: The team identified significant limitations in Unity Catalog's search capabilities, which only allow searching by table names, leading to inefficiencies in feature discovery and increased duplication of effort. The lack of enforced descriptions and tag taxonomies further exacerbates these issues, highlighting the need for enhanced search functionalities and duplicate detection mechanisms.

cases.query: What frustrations did the team face regarding schema changes from upstream sources?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to provide detailed error messages that s
As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to pause the stream on breaking schema ch

pain_points.description: Schema changes from upstream sources can cause Auto Loader to fail without clear diagnostic information, leading to significant downtime.
Auto Loader's schema evolution mode does not allow for graduated handling of breaking changes, forcing streams to fail entirely.
Checkpoint files accumulate in S3 without a clean way to prune them, leading to increased costs and risks of deleting active checkpoints.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to provide detailed error messages that specify which column changed and what the old and new types are, so that I can quickly diagnose and resolve schema issues without extended downtime.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of unclear error messages that prolong the resolution of schema-related failures.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to pause the stream on breaking schema changes and send alerts with details about the changes, so that I can take action before the stream fails.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for proactive handling of schema changes to prevent unplanned stream failures.

cases.summary: The team faced significant frustrations with schema changes from upstream sources, particularly with Auto Loader failing without clear diagnostics and lacking graduated handling for breaking changes. Additionally, the accumulation of checkpoint files in S3 poses a cost and management challenge. Addressing these pain points through improved error messaging and proactive alerts could enhance operational efficiency.

cases.query: What issues did the team encounter with Auto Loader during the last sprint?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to log schema changes without failing, so
As a Data Engineer, I want a built-in alerting system for Auto Loader stream hea

pain_points.description: Auto Loader failed due to a schema mismatch caused by an upstream Kafka producer changing a column type without notification.
Checkpoint files in Auto Loader are accumulating without a clean way to prune them, leading to increased costs.
Auto Loader's schema evolution mode is all-or-nothing, causing failures on type changes without a graduated acceptance of new columns.
Auto Loader streams can silently stall without alerts, leading to stale data being processed.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want Auto Loader to log schema changes without failing, so that I can handle them asynchronously and avoid stream interruptions.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of stream failures due to schema changes, allowing for smoother data processing.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a built-in alerting system for Auto Loader stream health, so that I can be notified of stalls and prevent stale data issues.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for proactive monitoring to ensure data freshness and reliability.

cases.summary: The team faced significant issues with Auto Loader, including failures due to schema mismatches and silent stalls in data processing. The need for improved schema evolution handling and built-in monitoring was highlighted as critical for maintaining data pipeline reliability.

cases.query: What are the main frustrations that Platform Admins face with current cluster policies?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to implement time-based and budget-based cluster pol
As a Platform Admin, I want a central admin console for managing multiple worksp
As a Platform Admin, I want a policy simulation tool, so that I can preview the

pain_points.description: Current cluster policies are static and do not allow for time-based or budget-based rules.
There is no central admin console for managing multiple workspaces, requiring separate logins for each.
Lack of visibility into specific job costs and team spending leads to difficulties in cost management.
No policy simulation tool to preview the effects of proposed changes before activation.
Enforcing tagging for cost allocation is challenging without native support for mandatory tags.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to implement time-based and budget-based cluster policies, so that I can enforce cost governance without blocking legitimate use.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for more expressive policies that adapt to usage patterns and budget constraints.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a central admin console for managing multiple workspaces, so that I can efficiently oversee cluster policies and costs without logging into each workspace individually.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the frustration of managing multiple workspaces separately, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a policy simulation tool, so that I can preview the impact of policy changes on past cluster usage before activating them.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better decision-making tools to avoid unintended consequences of policy changes.

cases.summary: Platform Admins face significant frustrations with static cluster policies that lack time and budget flexibility, as well as challenges in managing multiple workspaces without a central console. Additionally, visibility into specific costs and the enforcement of tagging for cost allocation remain critical pain points that hinder effective cost management.

cases.query: What pain points exist in documenting model promotion approvals for audits?

per_story.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Mode

pain_points.description: Current model promotion process relies on Slack messages for approvals, which do not provide a traceable audit record required by auditors.
Any user with EXECUTE privilege can promote models to Production without any required review or approval, leading to governance issues.
Promotion events lack detailed records of who reviewed and approved models, making it difficult to demonstrate compliance during audits.

user_stories.story: As an ML Engineer, I want a configurable promotion approval workflow in the Model Registry, so that I can ensure all model promotions are reviewed and documented for compliance audits.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for a formalized approval process that generates an auditable record, satisfying regulatory requirements.

cases.summary: The current model promotion process lacks a formal approval workflow, relying on informal methods that do not meet audit requirements. This leads to significant governance risks, as any user can promote models without oversight, and there is no traceable record of approvals. Implementing a structured approval process in the Model Registry is essential for compliance in regulated industries.

cases.query: What challenges do users encounter when trying to implement complex workflows with Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to define conditional execution paths in a Workflow D
As a Data Platform Lead, I want to create a single DAG that manages job dependen

pain_points.description: Limited conditional branching capabilities in Workflows lead to reliance on fragile workarounds.
Lack of native cross-workspace task dependencies complicates job scheduling across multiple regions.
Job run history retention is limited to 60 days, which can hinder compliance audits.
Dynamic parallelism is unsupported, making it difficult to process variable-length lists efficiently.
Jobs API versioning issues lead to breaking changes that disrupt job management as code.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want to define conditional execution paths in a Workflow DAG without hacking notebook exit values, so that I can implement complex workflows reliably and efficiently.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of having to use fragile workarounds for conditional branching, which can lead to errors and inefficiencies.

user_stories.story: As a Data Platform Lead, I want to create a single DAG that manages job dependencies across multiple workspaces, so that I can streamline job scheduling and reduce latency.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the challenge of coordinating jobs across different regions, which currently requires a fragile polling mechanism.

cases.summary: Users face significant challenges when implementing complex workflows in Databricks, particularly due to limited conditional branching capabilities and the lack of native cross-workspace dependencies. These issues lead to reliance on fragile workarounds and complicate job management, impacting efficiency and operational reliability.

cases.query: What unmet needs do ML engineers have regarding LLM agent evaluations?

cases.summary: The team faces significant challenges with cross-workspace polling due to the lack of native task dependencies in Databricks Workflows. A user story has been identified to advocate for the necessary feature to improve workflow reliability.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a fleet cost dashboard in the Account Console that aggregates and breaks down spend by workspace and team, so that I can produce cross-workspace cost reports in under 5 minutes without manual data assembly.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of spending excessive time on cost reporting by providing a centralized and automated solution.

cases.summary: The key issues with cost attribution across multiple workspaces include the time-consuming manual processes required for reporting and the challenges posed by inconsistent tagging conventions. A centralized cost dashboard could significantly streamline reporting efforts for Platform Admins.

cases.query: What issues did the team encounter during the UC migration related to data lineage?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to ensure that all lineage edges are transferred dur

pain_points.description: Lost lineage history for about 15% of the tables during the UC migration due to the migration tool not transferring lineage edges from the old metastore.
Manual reconstruction of lineage is required for tables in Unity Catalog that have no upstream lineage after migration.
The migration experience lacks adequate tooling and documentation, making it challenging for enterprises with legacy metastore history.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to ensure that all lineage edges are transferred during the Unity Catalog migration, so that I can maintain complete data lineage without manual reconstruction.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical issue of lost lineage history that complicates compliance and data governance.

cases.summary: The team faced significant challenges during the Unity Catalog migration, particularly with the loss of lineage history for a portion of tables, necessitating manual reconstruction. Additionally, the overall migration experience was hindered by inadequate tooling and documentation, impacting enterprises with existing legacy systems.

cases.query: How does the user rate the infrastructure of Mosaic AI, and what feature requests do they have?

cases.query: What specific changes are being proposed to reduce the need for user workarounds?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional task skips in Workflows to log the evalua
As a Data Engineer, I want explicit parameter validation at the start of every t
As a Platform Admin, I want a better deprecation process for the Jobs API Terraf

pain_points.description: Conditional task skips in Workflows do not log the evaluated condition, making it hard to understand why tasks are skipped.
Parameter passing across task types fails silently, leading to incorrect data being used in jobs.
Terraform updates to the Jobs API break job configurations without clear notifications, causing repeated issues.
Users spend excessive time building workarounds for platform limitations, indicating a lack of operational visibility and governance features.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional task skips in Workflows to log the evaluated condition expression and its result, so that I can understand why tasks were skipped without guessing.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of invisible task skips that complicate debugging.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want explicit parameter validation at the start of every task, so that I can avoid silent fallbacks to default values that lead to incorrect job execution.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical issue of silent failures in parameter passing that can result in significant data errors.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a better deprecation process for the Jobs API Terraform provider, so that I can avoid silent breaking changes that disrupt job configurations.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the recurring issue of unexpected Terraform updates causing job configuration failures.

cases.summary: The key findings highlight significant pain points related to operational visibility and the need for better logging and validation in Workflows. Users are facing challenges with silent failures and workarounds due to inadequate platform features, which impacts their efficiency and data integrity. Proposed user stories aim to address these issues directly by enhancing logging, validation, and configuration management.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users have regarding query performance diagnostics?

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the lack of guidance for users on how to resolve performance issues, empowering them to optimize their queries effectively.

cases.summary: Users of Unity Catalog face significant frustrations with lineage tracking, particularly due to gaps in lineage data and the inability to perform blast-radius analysis. These issues hinder compliance efforts and complicate data governance, especially during migrations from legacy systems.

cases.query: What limitations do users face with serverless compute in Databricks?

As a Data Scientist, I want to install packages with system-level dependencies i

pain_points.description: Query compilation time on complex models can take 20-30 seconds before any data is processed, slowing down development iteration.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the limitation of environment management in serverless compute that blocks certain workflows.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of wasted time due to long cluster startup latencies.

user_stories.story: As a Business Intelligence Analyst, I want reliable JDBC connectivity with Databricks SQL, so that I can maintain uninterrupted access to my data visualizations.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of random disconnections that disrupt the user experience with BI tools.

cases.summary: Users face significant limitations with serverless compute in Databricks, including long query compilation times, cost unpredictability, and environment management issues. Addressing these pain points could enhance user experience and efficiency.

cases.query: What suggestions were made to improve the Databricks Feature Store functionality?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want the Feature Store to validate point-in-time joins to
As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search capabilities in the Feature Store t
As a Data Scientist, I want native monitoring for feature distribution shifts in

pain_points.description: Point-in-time lookups in the Feature Store can lead to data leakage issues without proper validation.
Feature discovery is broken due to lack of search functionality by column name, description, or tags.
The Feature Store lacks native monitoring capabilities for tracking feature distribution shifts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want the Feature Store to validate point-in-time joins to prevent data leakage, so that I can ensure model integrity and avoid costly errors.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical issue of data leakage that can severely impact model performance and decision-making.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search capabilities in the Feature Store to find features by column name, description, or tags, so that I can avoid duplicating features and ensure consistency in model training.
user_stories.rationale: This story highlights the need for improved feature discoverability to enhance collaboration and efficiency within teams.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want native monitoring for feature distribution shifts in the Feature Store, so that I can receive alerts on potential data drift and maintain model performance.
user_stories.rationale: This story emphasizes the importance of proactive monitoring to ensure that models remain accurate and reliable over time.

cases.summary: The Databricks Feature Store has significant pain points related to point-in-time data leakage, inadequate feature discovery, and lack of monitoring capabilities. Users have expressed a need for built-in validation to prevent data issues, enhanced search functionality for better feature management, and native monitoring tools to track feature performance over time.

cases.query: What are the main challenges you face with job scheduling across multiple workspaces?

pain_points.description: No cross-workspace task dependencies in Databricks Workflows, forcing fragile polling workarounds.
Managing multiple workspaces requires logging into each one individually for administrative tasks, which is time-consuming.
Job scheduling across time zones is complicated due to the lack of native cross-workspace task dependencies.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want Databricks Workflows to support cross-workspace task dependencies, so that I can eliminate fragile polling workarounds and reduce latency in my data pipeline.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need for reliable job orchestration across multiple workspaces without introducing delays or failure risks.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a centralized admin console for managing multiple workspaces, so that I can perform administrative tasks without logging into each workspace individually.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the significant time burden of managing multiple workspaces and improves operational efficiency.

cases.summary: The main challenges with job scheduling across multiple workspaces include the lack of cross-workspace task dependencies in Databricks Workflows, leading to fragile polling workarounds and increased latency. Additionally, managing multiple workspaces requires individual logins for administrative tasks, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

cases.query: What feature requests were made in the recent sprint retrospective for Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to see column descriptions in the dbt catalog after mi

As a Data Scientist, I want to enforce a naming convention for MLflow experiment

pain_points.description: Column descriptions are missing from the dbt catalog after migration, leading to confusion among analysts.
Query performance variance is causing frustration among analysts, with inconsistent execution times for the same query.
Lack of version history in the UI leads to overwriting of work when multiple analysts edit the same query simultaneously.
Databricks notebooks lack collaborative features like real-time co-editing and version history, making them less effective than external tools.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to see column descriptions in the dbt catalog after migration, so that I can understand the data structure without confusion.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of missing column descriptions that leads to misunderstandings among analysts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Analyst, I want to receive notifications when query performance is affected by warehouse scaling, so that I can manage expectations and understand performance variance.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration caused by unpredictable query performance without clear explanations.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a version history feature in the SQL workspace, so that I can recover previous versions of queries and avoid overwriting others' work.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of losing work due to simultaneous edits by multiple analysts.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want real-time co-editing and version history in Databricks notebooks, so that I can collaborate more effectively with my team.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better collaboration features that are currently lacking in Databricks notebooks.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to view memory usage per object in my cluster, so that I can manage memory more effectively and avoid out-of-memory errors.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the issue of out-of-memory errors due to lack of visibility into cached DataFrames.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to search for features by column name in the Feature Store, so that I can easily find similar features and avoid duplication.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of poor feature discoverability that hinders efficient data science work.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to enforce a naming convention for MLflow experiments, so that I can easily locate and manage experiments over time.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the chaos in experiment naming that makes it difficult to find specific experiments.

cases.summary: The recent sprint retrospective highlighted critical pain points including missing column descriptions in the dbt catalog, inconsistent query performance, and lack of version history in the SQL workspace. User stories were generated to address these issues, focusing on improving clarity, collaboration, and feature discoverability within Databricks.

cases.query: What unmet needs do Platform Admins have regarding SLA monitoring in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to configure SLA alerts for all my Databricks pipeli
As a Platform Admin, I want a centralized 'Pipeline Health' dashboard that displ

pain_points.description: Platform Admins currently lack a built-in mechanism for SLA monitoring and alerting on pipeline performance, leading to significant manual effort and potential oversight of stalled pipelines.
Existing alerting mechanisms only notify on job failures, not on delays or performance issues, which can result in unaddressed SLA breaches.
There is no centralized dashboard for monitoring the health of multiple pipelines, forcing teams to recreate dashboards manually and increasing the risk of missed alerts.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to configure SLA alerts for all my Databricks pipelines, so that I can receive timely notifications if any pipeline fails to meet its performance requirements without building custom monitoring solutions.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for automated monitoring and alerting, reducing manual effort and the risk of oversight.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a centralized 'Pipeline Health' dashboard that displays the status of all my pipelines, so that I can quickly assess compliance with SLAs and identify issues without logging into multiple workspaces.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain point of operational visibility and the need for efficient monitoring across multiple pipelines.

cases.summary: Platform Admins face significant challenges with SLA monitoring due to the lack of built-in alerting mechanisms and centralized dashboards in Databricks. These gaps lead to increased manual effort and the risk of undetected pipeline issues, highlighting the need for automated solutions to improve operational visibility and efficiency.

cases.query: What frustrations do users have regarding the current cost attribution capabilities in Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databrick
As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce mandatory tagging policies for clusters a

pain_points.description: Inability to break down total DBU spend by team, pipeline, or business unit, leading to poor cost visibility.
Approximately 15% of compute runs untagged, resulting in incomplete cost attribution.
Serverless SQL warehouse costs cannot be tagged at resource creation time, creating a permanent gap in tag-based attribution.
Manual assembly of cost reports from various sources is time-consuming and inefficient.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a first-class Cost Attribution view in the Databricks Account Console, so that I can produce a per-team cost breakdown for any 30-day window in under 5 minutes, with zero custom SQL or data exports required.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for improved cost visibility and efficiency in reporting.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enforce mandatory tagging policies for clusters and jobs, so that I can eliminate unattributed spend and ensure accurate cost attribution.
user_stories.rationale: This story directly addresses the pain point of untagged compute runs affecting cost visibility.

cases.summary: Users express significant frustration with the lack of detailed cost attribution capabilities in Databricks, particularly the inability to break down spending by team or project. The manual effort required to compile cost reports and the prevalence of untagged compute resources further exacerbate these issues, highlighting a critical need for enhanced cost management features.

cases.query: What unmet needs do users express regarding data governance in Unity Catalog?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a robust bulk migration tool for Unity Catalog that h
As a Compliance Officer, I want complete column-level lineage data for all opera
As a Platform Admin, I want stronger metadata isolation features in Unity Catalo
As a Data Engineer, I want automated tools to identify and tag PHI columns, so t
As a Data Engineer, I want an automated audit trail for model approvals, so that

pain_points.description: The tooling for bulk migrations to Unity Catalog is immature, leading to manual re-tagging of column-level tags and custom properties post-migration.
Column-level lineage data has gaps for certain operations, making it difficult to provide complete lineage information for compliance.
There is a lack of strong metadata isolation within a single metastore, posing a risk of metadata leakage in multi-client environments.
The absence of enforcement for meaningful column descriptions leads to a lack of useful metadata in the data catalog.
Manual tagging of PHI columns is time-consuming and lacks automated support, leading to compliance risks.
Audit records for model approvals are incomplete, requiring manual reconstruction of approval history.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a robust bulk migration tool for Unity Catalog that handles edge cases, so that I can migrate tables without extensive manual re-tagging.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual re-tagging post-migration, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

user_stories.story: As a Compliance Officer, I want complete column-level lineage data for all operations, so that I can accurately track the flow of sensitive data in our system.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the gaps in lineage data that hinder compliance efforts.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want stronger metadata isolation features in Unity Catalog, so that I can ensure complete data isolation for each client without managing multiple workspaces.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the risk of metadata leakage in multi-client environments.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want automated tools to identify and tag PHI columns, so that I can ensure compliance without manual effort.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of manual tagging, which is inefficient and prone to oversight.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want an automated audit trail for model approvals, so that I can easily provide compliance documentation without manual reconstruction.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the inefficiency of reconstructing approval history from Slack messages.

cases.summary: Users express significant unmet needs regarding data governance in Unity Catalog, particularly around migration tooling, lineage completeness, and metadata isolation. The lack of automated support for tagging and auditing further complicates compliance efforts, highlighting the need for enhanced features in these areas.

cases.query: What factors could improve user satisfaction with Databricks?

cases.query: What are the unmet needs regarding error handling in job configurations?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional task skips to log the evaluated condition
As a Data Engineer, I want explicit error notifications for missing required par
As a Platform Admin, I want to receive notifications of breaking changes in the

pain_points.description: Conditional task skips in Workflows do not log the evaluated condition, leading to confusion about why tasks are skipped.
Silent fallback to default parameters in job configurations can lead to significant data issues without any error notification.
Breaking changes in the Jobs API schema are not communicated effectively, resulting in silent failures and production job misconfigurations.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want conditional task skips to log the evaluated condition and its result in the job run event history, so that I can understand why tasks were skipped without confusion.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better visibility into task execution and error handling in Workflows.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want explicit error notifications for missing required parameters in job configurations, so that I can avoid silent data issues and ensure data integrity.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the critical need to prevent silent failures that can lead to significant data correctness issues.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to receive notifications of breaking changes in the Jobs API schema, so that I can proactively manage job configurations and prevent production failures.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better communication regarding API changes to avoid silent misconfigurations.

cases.summary: The analysis reveals significant unmet needs in error handling related to job configurations in Databricks. Key issues include a lack of visibility into conditional task skips, silent parameter fallbacks leading to data integrity risks, and insufficient communication regarding breaking API changes. Addressing these pain points through structured user stories can enhance the reliability and usability of Databricks workflows.

cases.query: What problems arose from multiple users working on the same query?

per_story.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to see presence indicators for queries being ed
As an Analytics Engineer, I want conflict detection when saving a query that has
As an Analytics Engineer, I want to access a version history of saved queries, s

pain_points.description: Multiple analysts editing the same saved query leads to silent overwrites without any warning or conflict detection.
There is no version history for saved queries, making it impossible to recover lost content after an overwrite.
Lack of presence indicators means users cannot see if another analyst is currently editing a query, leading to uncoordinated edits.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to see presence indicators for queries being edited by other users, so that I can avoid overwriting their changes.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of uncoordinated edits that lead to lost work.

user_stories.story: As an Analytics Engineer, I want to access a version history of saved queries, so that I can recover previous versions if my work is overwritten.
user_stories.rationale: This story resolves the problem of lost content due to overwrites.

cases.summary: The key problems identified include silent overwrites of queries when multiple analysts edit simultaneously, the absence of version history for saved queries, and the lack of presence indicators for users currently editing queries. These issues lead to significant frustration and inefficiencies in collaborative work among analytics teams.

cases.query: How much time is wasted in daily setup rituals for Databricks?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want to restore my notebook environment from a snapshot,

pain_points.description: Data Scientists waste 20-30 minutes daily on setup rituals due to cluster termination wiping in-memory state.

cases.summary: Data Scientists face a major pain point with daily setup rituals that consume 20-30 minutes due to cluster terminations. Implementing a feature to restore notebook environments from snapshots could significantly reduce this wasted time, enhancing productivity.

cases.query: Can you summarize the issues with MLflow experiment management?

per_story.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define retention policies for MLflow experiments,

pain_points.description: MLflow has no native concept of experiment or artifact retention, leading to indefinite accumulation of artifacts and high storage costs.

Storage costs for MLflow artifacts are not surfaced in Databricks cost reporting, making it difficult to attribute costs accurately.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define retention policies for MLflow experiments, so that I can automatically clean up stale artifacts and reduce storage costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for managing artifact accumulation and controlling costs effectively.

cases.summary: The key issues with MLflow experiment management include the lack of native retention policies leading to high storage costs, difficulties in bulk deletion of experiments, and challenges in distinguishing relevant artifacts. Additionally, the absence of cost attribution for artifact storage complicates financial oversight for teams managing numerous experiments.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want breaking changes in the Jobs API to be versioned and announced with a minimum 90-day deprecation notice, so that I can avoid unexpected failures in production jobs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the need for better communication and planning around API changes to prevent disruptions in critical workflows.

cases.summary: Recent updates to the Jobs API Terraform provider have caused significant disruptions for Data Engineers due to unannounced breaking changes in the task dependency schema. These changes led to production job failures and data quality issues, highlighting the need for a more robust deprecation policy and better communication from Databricks.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of navigating a growing number of dashboards and ensures users can find trusted data sources.

cases.summary: The executive-facing revenue dashboard has significant issues with stale data rendering, which undermines trust in the platform. Additionally, the lack of a tagging system for dashboards complicates navigation for analysts, while accidental selection of the wrong warehouse leads to slow query performance. Addressing these pain points is crucial for improving user experience and data reliability.

cases.query: What challenges do Databricks Platform Admins encounter when monitoring costs across multiple workspaces?

cases.summary: Platform Admins face significant challenges in monitoring costs across multiple Databricks workspaces due to the lack of a centralized management console, leading to time-consuming manual reporting processes. cases.query: What feature requests were discussed in relation to the cost attribution feature?

cases.query: What frustrations do users have regarding prompt versioning in Mosaic AI?

As an ML Engineer, I want to run the same prompt against multiple models side-by

Prompt iterations in the Playground are ephemeral and lost when the session ends, resulting in a lack of history.
There is no ability to run the same prompt against multiple models side-by-side, making comparison difficult.
Output quality evaluation is manual and subjective, lacking statistical tracking.
There is no integration between Playground iterations and MLflow, leading to disconnected tracking of prompt and model experiments.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of disconnected tracking between prompts and models, ensuring better audit trails.

cases.summary: Users of Mosaic AI are frustrated with the lack of prompt versioning, leading to confusion about which versions are live in production and potential quality drops. cases.query: What frustrations do users have with the Data Lake ingestion process?

per_story.story: As a Data Engineer, I want the DLT UI to provide warnings about using directory
As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation on watermarking and late data ha

pain_points.description: Lack of clear guidance or warnings in the DLT UI regarding best practices for ingestion modes, leading to inefficient processing times.
Sparse documentation on watermarking and late data handling in DLT, resulting in silent data loss and extended diagnosis times.
Full refresh of DLT pipelines drops and recreates all tables, causing long reprocessing times and slowing down iteration speed.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want better documentation on watermarking and late data handling in DLT, so that I can prevent silent data loss and reduce diagnosis time.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of losing data without warnings, which complicates the data pipeline management.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want the ability to perform partial refreshes on DLT pipelines, so that I can update specific tables without affecting the entire pipeline.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the operational challenge of long reprocessing times that slow down development.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want DLT event logs to include detailed error messages and stack traces, so that I can quickly identify the root cause of pipeline failures.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of inadequate error logging, which hinders troubleshooting and trust in pipeline outputs.

user_stories.story: As a Data Engineer, I want a local testing capability for DLT, so that I can test changes without incurring cloud compute costs.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the frustration of needing to run expensive cloud resources for testing, which is inefficient.

cases.summary: Users face significant frustrations with the Data Lake ingestion process, including lack of guidance in the DLT UI, sparse documentation on critical features, and inadequate error logging. These issues lead to inefficient processing, data loss, and slow iteration speeds, highlighting the need for improved support and features in Databricks' ingestion tools.

No in-product guidance or plain-language explanations for slow queries, leaving users without actionable insights.

Enhancements like visual breakdowns and performance summaries are essential for improving user experience and trust in the platform.

cases.query: What are the key pain points related to data validation in the churn model?

cases.query: What decisions were made to address the pain points in the last sprint?

cases.query: What improvements are needed for the Unity Catalog to enhance usability?

per_story.story: As a Data Scientist, I want full-text search across feature column names, descri
As a Platform Admin, I want a single-pane-of-glass admin view for managing all w
As a Data Scientist, I want duplicate detection on feature creation, so that I c

pain_points.description: Current Unity Catalog search only matches table names, not column names, descriptions, or tags, making it difficult to find existing features.
Feature tables lack enforced descriptions or tag taxonomy, leading to empty descriptions and ineffective text search.
Lineage data has gaps, particularly for certain operations, making it challenging to provide complete lineage information for compliance.
No duplicate detection when creating new feature definitions, leading to wasted engineering time on duplicate features.
Platform administrators lack a centralized view for managing multiple workspaces, complicating governance and oversight.

user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of having to manually browse feature tables to find existing features, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

user_stories.story: As a Platform Admin, I want a single-pane-of-glass admin view for managing all workspaces, so that I can efficiently oversee governance and permissions without logging into each workspace separately.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the challenge of managing multiple workspaces, which currently requires significant manual effort.

user_stories.story: As a Data Scientist, I want duplicate detection on feature creation, so that I can avoid rebuilding features that already exist and save engineering time.
user_stories.rationale: This story addresses the pain of wasting time on duplicate feature engineering, which is a significant issue for teams.

cases.summary: The Unity Catalog needs significant improvements in search functionality, lineage completeness, and feature management to enhance usability. Key pain points include ineffective search capabilities, lack of enforced metadata, and challenges in managing multiple workspaces. Addressing these issues through user stories focused on search enhancements, centralized administration, and duplicate detection can lead to more efficient workflows for data scientists and platform administrators.

MLflow lacks maturity in model governance features, requiring manual processes.
